title: Wave, Coverflow, Orbits, Reveal & Progress
description: Five live UI demos: distance-stagger wave grid, CSS coverflow scroll, SVG text orbits, Fluent cursor reveal, and scroll-driven progress ring.

drops.title: Wave Cascade Stagger
drops.description: Grid tiles animate outward from click point with delays computed from Euclidean distance — the CSS sibling-index() technique.
drops.concept: Click any tile and a wave of animation ripples outward. Each cell's animation-delay is set proportional to its distance from the clicked origin, matching what CSS sibling-index() enables natively in supporting browsers. Five distinct animation modes: burst, ripple, flip, snake, and diagonal.
drops.source: CSS sibling-index() Working Group specification + CSS Tricks stagger patterns

interaction.mouse: Click any tile to trigger wave
interaction.click: Mode buttons to switch animation style

drops.title: CSS Coverflow Gallery
drops.description: 3D coverflow gallery using CSS scroll-snap + animation-timeline: view(inline) for rotateY and scale — no JS for the 3D effect.
drops.concept: A horizontal scroll gallery where each card applies a CSS keyframe animation linked to animation-timeline: view(inline). As cards scroll through the center, they transition from rotateY(55deg) scale(0.65) at entry to rotateY(0) scale(1) at center to rotateY(-55deg) scale(0.65) at exit — pure CSS, no IntersectionObserver needed.
drops.source: CSS Scroll-Driven Animations Level 1 specification — view() timeline in inline axis

interaction.click: Click dots to jump to card

drops.title: SVG Text Orbits
drops.description: Six rings of text flowing along circular SVG paths using textPath + SMIL animate startOffset — no CSS, no JavaScript.
drops.concept: Each text element is attached to a circular SVG path via textPath href. SMIL animate elements on startOffset drive the text around the circles at different speeds and in alternating directions. The innermost ring moves fastest; the outermost slowest — creating a planetary orbit feel purely in SVG markup.
drops.source: SVG 1.1 textPath element + SMIL animation spec, demonstrated by Chris Coyier / CSS-Tricks

interaction.mouse: Scroll to zoom, click center to pause/resume

drops.title: Cursor Reveal Grid
drops.description: Cards that independently glow at the exact cursor position via CSS --cx/--cy custom properties — Microsoft Fluent Reveal Highlight.
drops.concept: On mousemove, each card receives its own --cx and --cy CSS custom property set to the cursor position relative to that card. A ::before pseudo-element paints a radial-gradient centered at those coordinates, creating an independent glow under the cursor for every card simultaneously. The border subtly glows too via a gradient-fill border technique using mask-composite.
drops.source: Microsoft Fluent Design System 'Reveal Highlight' pattern — fluent2.microsoft.com

interaction.mouse: Move cursor over cards to see independent glow

drops.title: Scroll Progress Indicators
drops.description: CSS animation-timeline: scroll() drives both a linear progress bar and an SVG stroke-dashoffset ring — compositor-thread, zero JS scroll listeners.
drops.concept: A reading progress demo with two indicators: a top linear bar (transform: scaleX) and a bottom-right circular SVG ring (stroke-dashoffset). Both link to animation-timeline: scroll() — the CSS scroll-driven animations API that ties keyframe progress directly to the page scroll position, eliminating JavaScript scroll listeners entirely and running on the compositor thread.
drops.source: CSS Scroll-Driven Animations Level 1 spec — scroll() timeline function

interaction.mouse: Scroll the article to see both indicators fill
